pr.title: Use `.L_` prefix for unnamed local labels
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Standardized unnamed local labels to use the `.L_` prefix via the `melee-fix-labels` tooling. The PR body reports this can be reproduced by checking out commit `f81423d63d68eae1625a462b822a7f943312598e` and running `cargo run -rp melee-fix-labels`, and that the change reduced the map file from 194,386 to 61,896 lines, a 68.15% reduction. No diff, changed-file list, or review comments were present in the provided slice, so exact file-level edits are not available.
what_changed: Changed the convention for unnamed local labels to use a `.L_` prefix.
The PR body indicates the change is generated or reproducible with `cargo run -rp melee-fix-labels`.
The PR body says other branches can be synced by checking out the `tools` directory and running the same command.
The PR body warns that commit `f81423d63d68eae1625a462b822a7f943312598e` must be patched in or an internal linker error will occur.
Reported impact: map file size dropped from 194,386 lines to 61,896 lines, a 68.15% reduction.
smart_moves: Used a tooling-driven label normalization workflow rather than hand-editing labels, making the cleanup reproducible across branches.
Documented the exact command needed to reproduce the change: `cargo run -rp melee-fix-labels`.
Called out the required prerequisite commit to avoid an internal linker error, which is important for anyone replaying the transformation.
Quantified the practical benefit with a map-file line-count reduction, showing that the naming convention had concrete output-size/readability effects.
decomp_lessons: For large-scale assembly-label convention changes, prefer deterministic tooling so the same cleanup can be applied consistently across branches.
Local/unnamed labels should follow a convention that keeps linker and map output manageable.
When a tooling pass depends on a compiler/linker/toolchain fix, document the prerequisite commit or patch explicitly.
Measure and report downstream effects such as map-file size when making naming or label-generation changes; this helps justify non-functional cleanup.
naming_conventions: Unnamed local labels should use the `.L_` prefix.
The `.L_` prefix appears to be intended for labels that should be treated as local/unnamed rather than exported or map-noisy symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `cargo run -rp melee-fix-labels` to apply the unnamed local label prefix normalization.
For syncing other branches, the PR body says to check out the `tools` directory and rerun the label-fixing command.
Patch in or include commit `f81423d63d68eae1625a462b822a7f943312598e` before running the tool to avoid an internal linker error.
Reducing nonessential labels in map output can make matching and symbol auditing easier by removing large amounts of local-label noise.
follow_up_queries: Find the implementation of `melee-fix-labels` to understand exactly how `.L_` labels are detected and rewritten.
Inspect commit `f81423d63d68eae1625a462b822a7f943312598e` for the linker-error fix required by this workflow.
Search later PRs for `.L_` or `melee-fix-labels` to see whether the convention was refined or enforced in CI.
Compare map output before and after this PR to identify which symbol categories were removed or collapsed.
